segments.text: These are the regulations you are to present to them:
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: If you buy a Hebrew slave, he is to work for you for six years. But in the seventh year, he is to be freed without having to pay anything.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: If he was single when he came, he is to leave single. If he had a wife when he came, she is to leave with him.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: If his master provides him a wife and she has children with him, the woman and her children shall belong to her master, and only the man shall be freed.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: However, if the slave formally states, ‘I love my master and my wife and children; I do not want to be freed,’
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: then his master is to take him before the judges. [^49] Then he shall have him st and against the door or doorpost and use a metal tool to make a hole in his ear. Then he shall work for his master for life.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: If a man sells his daughter as a slave, she is not to be freed in the same way as male slaves.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: If the man who chose her for himself[^50] is not pleased with her, he must let her be bought back. He is not permitted to sell her to foreigners, since he has been unfair to her.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: If he chooses to give her to his son, he must treat her as a daughter.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: If he takes another woman, he must not reduce the food and clothing allowances and marital rights of the first.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: If he doesn't give her these three things, she is free to leave without paying anything.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Anyone who hits and kills someone else must be executed.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: However, if it wasn't intentional and God let it happen, then I will arrange a place for you where they can run to and be safe.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: But if someone deliberately plans and purposely kills another, you must take them away from my altar[^51] and execute them.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Anyone who hits their father or mother must be executed.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Anyone who kidnaps someone else must be executed, whether the victim is sold or is still in their possession.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Anyone who despises their father or mother must be executed.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: If men are fighting and one hits the other with a stone or with his fist, and the injured man doesn't die but has to stay in bed,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: and then gets up and walks around outside with his walking stick, then the one who hit him won't be punished. Even so, he must still compensate the man for the time lost from his work and make sure that he's completely healed.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Anyone who hits their male or female slave with a rod, and the slave dies as a result, must be punished.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: However, if after a day or two the slave gets better, the owner won't be punished because the slave is their property.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: If men who are fighting happen to hit a pregnant woman so she gives birth prematurely, [^52] but no serious injury occurs, he must be fined whatever amount the woman's husband demands and as permitted by the judges.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: But if a serious injury does occur, then you must pay a life for a life,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: an eye for an eye, a tooth for a tooth, a hand for a hand, a foot for a foot,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: a burn for a burn, a wound for a wound, and a bruise for a bruise.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Anyone who hits their male or female slave in the eye and blinds them must free the slave as compensation for the eye.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Anyone who knocks out the tooth of their male or female slave must free the slave as compensation for the tooth.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: If an ox uses its horns to kill a man or woman, the ox must be stoned to death, and its meat must not be eaten. But the owner of the ox won't be punished.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: But if the ox has repeatedly hurt people with its horns, and its owner has been warned but still doesn't keep it under control, and it kills a man or woman, then the ox must be stoned to death and its owner must also be executed.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: But if instead the payment of compensation is required, the owner may buy back his or her life by paying the full amount of compensation demanded.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: If the ox uses its horns and kills a son or a daughter the same rule is applicable.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: If the ox uses its horns and kills a male or female slave, the owner of the ox must pay thirty shekels of silver to the slave's master, and the ox must be stoned to death.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: If someone removes the cover of a cistern or digs one and doesn't cover it, and an ox or a donkey falls into it,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: the owner of the pit must pay compensation to the animal's owner and keep the dead animal.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: If someone's ox injures another's ox and it dies, they must sell the live one and share money received; they must also share the dead animal.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: But if it was known that the ox had repeatedly hurt people with its horns, and its owner had been warned but still didn't keep it under control, full compensation must be paid, ox for ox, but the owner can keep the dead animal.
segments.speaker: The LORD
